raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The section makes an interpretive causal claim—that weaker inter-rater agreement on clinical utility stems from 'differing rating thresholds'—that requires logical scrutiny; the aggregation-by-mean choice and the three-rater design also have consistency implications worth checking.","R1.3":"Specific numerical claims (3.8 vs 4.2 factual accuracy; alpha range 0.41–0.58) must be traceable to reported data; the assertion that the gap is 'consistent with what several prior studies have been reporting' is an evidence-claim that is unsupported within the text.","R1.6":"All three paragraphs are written as run-on sentences with heavy 'and'-chaining and no subordination; sentence-level revision is clearly needed and is a plausible primary intent of a generic 'review' request.","R1.7":"MIMIC-CXR corpus is named but not cited; Krippendorff's alpha is invoked without a reference; 'several prior studies on transformer-based report generation systems' is asserted without any citations, creating a scholarship gap."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review prioritize editorial and writing quality (sentence clarity, prose flow, run-on repair) as its load-bearing output, or methodological and content critique (logical soundness of design choices, evidence-claim alignment, citation coverage)?"}

rationale.R1.2: The section makes an interpretive causal claim—that weaker inter-rater agreement on clinical utility stems from 'differing rating thresholds'—that requires logical scrutiny; the aggregation-by-mean choice and the three-rater design also have consistency implications worth checking.
rationale.R1.3: Specific numerical claims (3.8 vs 4.2 factual accuracy; alpha range 0.41–0.58) must be traceable to reported data; the assertion that the gap is 'consistent with what several prior studies have been reporting' is an evidence-claim that is unsupported within the text.
rationale.R1.6: All three paragraphs are written as run-on sentences with heavy 'and'-chaining and no subordination; sentence-level revision is clearly needed and is a plausible primary intent of a generic 'review' request.
rationale.R1.7: MIMIC-CXR corpus is named but not cited; Krippendorff's alpha is invoked without a reference; 'several prior studies on transformer-based report generation systems' is asserted without any citations, creating a scholarship gap.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize editorial and writing quality (sentence clarity, prose flow, run-on repair) as its load-bearing output, or methodological and content critique (logical soundness of design choices, evidence-claim alignment, citation coverage)?
